content.question: To what extent is the measured 1-2 percentage point rise in SEN identification (2010-2020) attributable to underlying prevalence change versus improved diagnostic practices and greater awareness?
content.reduction_path: Conduct a retrospective decomposition study comparing: (a) incidence rates in a cohort followed from birth through school entry with documented contemporaneous diagnostic practice and funding policy changes; (b) comparative analysis of SEN identification rates and diagnostic protocols in similar Hungarian schools before/after introduction of new screening tools or awareness campaigns; (c) qualitative interviews with school psychologists and teachers on how diagnostic thresholds have shifted
